notes: Opening work notes for plaintiff, arb-ex08-20260630035158. Opportunity openings:plaintiff; only final filing action is record_opening_statement; no evidence admission available. I inspected get_case/list_evidence and read all five initial packet files by evidence_id. Visible record: ev_90fa205907f7_market-page gives Polymarket rule: any message or note written by Epstein, intended as suicide note/final message/equivalent, public by listed date; qualifying disclosure can be official/leaked/otherwise; resolution source is consensus of credible reporting. It identifies the May 8 market and disputed UMA status, but the arbitration asks factual condition, not market result. ev_e631aed74f36_primary-evidence preserves Reuters/NPR/BBC/Guardian excerpts: strong for public release before May 8 and credible reporting of a purported/alleged Epstein suicide note, with text about choosing time to say goodbye; also strong adverse caveats that the note was not independently authenticated, DOJ/court did not vouch authorship, and intent/meaning are uncertain. ev_b3b39b867ad2_official-source-record confirms no local SDNY PDF/order/note image in packet; reporting says Judge Karas released/unsealed the note after NYT request, and the rule does not require official release. ev_d6c0d4659002_secondary-reporting identifies NYT leads but full NYT not captured. ev_2680dc66be4e_unresolved-record-gaps flags missing SDNY docket/order/image, missing full NYT, unresolved consensus/authorship/intent, mutable Polymarket dispute state. Native investigation: searched Reuters/NPR/BBC/Guardian terms; opened PBS/AP page at https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/judge-releases-note-that-cellmate-says-he-found-after-epsteins-suspected-suicide-attempt, Courthouse News at https://www.courthousenews.com/supposed-epstein-jail-cell-suicide-note-unsealed/, and FOX29 full-text page at https://www.fox29.com/news/epsteins-purported-suicide-note-unsealed-judge-read-full-text. These are source targets for later admission, not record evidence yet. Plaintiff theory: rule requires credible public reporting, not conclusive authentication; multiple credible outlets before May 8 described a court-released document as a purported/alleged Epstein suicide note/final-style message. Defense theory: hedging/non-authentication/chain-of-custody and uncertain intent may defeat credible reporting of authorship and intent. Next admission targets: AP/PBS or Reuters article text, Reuters/court handout image if accessible, Courthouse News linked handwritten note/NYT request, and adverse caveat passages.

Opening:defendant work notes. Objective: oppose proposition that by May 8, 2026 a message/note credibly reported to have been written by Jeffrey Epstein and intended as suicide note/final/final-equivalent was publicly available. Opportunity allows only record_opening_statement; no evidence submission. Record scan: get_case/list_evidence show five case-packet files only; no submitted evidence/exhibits. Read all five files after stat_evidence. Market-page says qualifying note must be credibly reported to have been written by Epstein and intended as suicide note/final message/equivalent; public availability may be official/leak/other; resolution source consensus of credible reporting; May 8 market identified despite May 31 group API end date. Primary-evidence file preserves Reuters/NPR/BBC/Guardian excerpts: they support public release/unsealing by May 6/7 and describe a purported/alleged/apparent suicide note; important adverse caveats include judge did not vouch for authenticity or chain of custody, NPR says DOJ had not authenticated and was seeing it for first time, BBC/Guardian did not independently verify authorship, BBC notes uncertainty over meaning. Official-source record likewise says rule does not require official source but official release did not decide authorship or intent; packet lacks SDNY docket PDF/order/note image. Secondary-reporting and unresolved-gaps files identify missing full NYT capture, docket/order/note image, unresolved consensus/authorship/intent, mutable Polymarket status. Native investigation before opening: searched Reuters/DOJ/SDNY terms and found official SDNY PDF at https://www.nysd.uscourts.gov/sites/default/files/2026-05/16cr832%20-%20Tartaglione%20Order%20-%2026.5.6.pdf. Opened it; observed Case No. 16-CR-832 (KMK), Judge Kenneth M. Karas order dated May 6, 2026, granting NYT request as to alleged suicide note purportedly authored by Epstein and directing clerk to docket copy/unseal original material placed in vault May 20, 2021; reserves remaining materials. This is not yet admitted but is a prime next-phase evidence target. Other leads: Reuters Connect image/caption, NPR/BBC/Guardian full pages, NYT authentication follow-up. Defense theory: public availability is likely strongest plaintiff element, but the burden requires credible reporting of authorship and intent, not merely public release of an alleged/purported document. The reporting consensus is hedged and preserves lack of authentication, chain of custody, independent verification, and uncertain meaning. Opening will acknowledge likely public-release evidence while arguing the decisive issues are authorship/intent/credible-reporting threshold and missing source-chain materials.

Argument work notes for plaintiff, arb-ex08-20260630035158. Refreshed get_case/list_evidence: record now contains both openings and same five case-packet evidence files; no party evidence before plaintiff argument. Defense opening concedes substantial public description before May 8 but contests authorship/intent through hedging, lack of authentication, chain of custody, and missing official SDNY PDF/image. Evidence submitted this phase: ev_96ddd443637f_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-96ddd443637f (AP/PBS report by Associated Press, published May 6/updated May 7, captured text; supports release by Judge Karas after NYT request, note text, Reuters/SDNY image caption, and adverse caveats); ev_8edf2fc4d79f_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-8edf2fc4d79f (Courthouse News, May 6, court-focused corroboration; describes letter purported to be suicide note, NYT request path, note text); ev_a1d333b3c55c_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-a1d333b3c55c (FOX Local/FOX4, May 6, concise full-text capture, federal judge unsealed purported suicide note, source based on government court documents/NYT/AP). Additional investigation: fetched candidate image URLs. PBS/Reuters/SDNY image URL https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/05/2026-05-07T012353Z_1832433893_RC2T3LAEQVV7_RTRMADP_3_USA-EPSTEIN-791x1024.jpg is JPEG, 97,457 bytes, sha256 65d5d34ab0f0784034093689451aaf1f9ae1f3fa91d42dd139fd2b5bcee91888. FOX image URL https://images.foxtv.com/static.livenowfox.com/www.livenowfox.com/content/uploads/2026/05/932/524/b64b20b1-alleged-suicide-note-epstein.jpg?ve=1&tl=1 is JPEG, 69,027 bytes, sha256 2f01f5dae4d666f0cfe4374c7f1e9758ea672c81cc37f70dee0836a865a4a9f9. Attempted full base64 via terminal but output truncated; did not risk partial binary upload. CourtListener/SDNY official path searched; no docket/order/note PDF successfully captured in time; Courthouse fetched HTML did not expose document hrefs. Merits theory: rule asks for credible reporting, not conclusive authentication. AP/PBS, Courthouse, and FOX Local are all public before May 8 and characterize a court-released/unsealed note as purported/alleged Epstein suicide note/final-message equivalent, with farewell language. Caveats go to weight but do not defeat preponderance under credible-reporting language. Filing should acknowledge missing official PDF/image and adverse authentication evidence, then argue the standard is satisfied.

Argument:defendant notes. Opportunity permits submit_evidence and submit_argument. Rescanned get_case/list_evidence and read plaintiff's three new evidence submissions. Plaintiff evidence: ev_96ddd443637f AP/PBS supports public availability and note text/caption, but states unclear who wrote note; note absent from government death reports and DOJ recent files; jail records say Epstein denied self-harm; officials found a separate handwritten note at death that did not appear to be a suicide note and was a grievance list. ev_8edf2fc4d79f Courthouse News supports public availability/characterization but uses purported/believed wording. ev_a1d333b3c55c FOX Local supports public availability/full text but says note may have been written by Epstein and relies on NYT/AP/court docs. Native investigation: downloaded official SDNY order PDF from https://www.nysd.uscourts.gov/sites/default/files/2026-05/16cr832%20-%20Tartaglione%20Order%20-%2026.5.6.pdf to /aar-codex/sdny-tartaglione-order-2026-05-06.pdf; size 128,992 bytes; sha256 7225441cb802084e2b9485ebc2c6d246fbfa08e109fe650cb14f445dc4745ca0. Local extraction issues: pdftotext not installed; pypdf not installed; pip unavailable. Used web PDF text extraction. Submitted ev_c2a2fd25e0b1 as official order extraction/summary; it proves court ordered unsealing/docketing but frames item as alleged/purported/reported and does not authenticate handwriting, chain of custody, or intent. Further searches: exact DOJ quote and NYT/authentication terms. Submitted ev_e650545a6c2b AP report that DOJ said it never had the note, note had not yet been authenticated, and this was first time DOJ was seeing it; source chain runs through Tartaglione and sealed attorney-client proceedings. Adverse check: searched AP handwriting analysis and LA Times echo/phrase sources. Submitted ev_e64fc0af3e88 AP handwriting analysis because it is strongest adverse authorship source. It reports experts saw or appeared to see common authorship between released note and post-death grievance note, but none could definitively say Epstein wrote notes due partly to lack of confirmed handwriting samples; comparison note was grievance not suicide; one expert would not eliminate Tartaglione but did not suggest he wrote it. Argument theory: concede timing/public availability likely. Press that proposition requires credible reporting of Epstein authorship and suicide/final-message intent. Official order and DOJ source defeat any claim of official authentication. AP handwriting improves authorship but not definitive Epstein authorship and also supports intent ambiguity because the matching comparison note was not a suicide note. Intent proof is mostly later characterization plus ambiguous phrase. Filing will offer D-1 SDNY order, D-2 AP DOJ non-authentication, D-3 AP handwriting analysis, and use plaintiff AP/PBS caveats and packet evidence. Technical report will record search ledger/extraction failures.

Rebuttal work notes for plaintiff. Refreshed evidence list and read defendant’s three new evidence items: ev_c2a2fd25e0b1_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-c2a2fd25e0b1 (official SDNY order), ev_e650545a6c2b_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-e650545a6c2b (AP DOJ unauthenticated/first-seen article), and ev_e64fc0af3e88_submitted-evidence-06-defendant-e64fc0af3e88 (AP handwriting analysis). Targeted rebuttal: D1/official order helps plaintiff because it is dated May 6 and orders docketing/unsealing of the note from the vault; it identifies the item as an alleged suicide note purportedly authored by Epstein. The court need not authenticate under market rule. D2 has DOJ caveat but also confirms note made public, NYT petition/Judge Karas/DOJ non-objection, goodbye-language text, suspected July 23 suicide attempt, friction marks/skin irritation, suicide watch, and possible 2021 privilege bar explaining DOJ silence. D3 is strongest plaintiff rebuttal: AP got three forensic document examiners to compare the released note with the known/public jail note found after Epstein’s death; they found or appeared to find common authorship, one said same writer, and article reports phrases echoed Epstein’s past emails. Lack of definitive Epstein identification is not fatal because proposition requires credible reporting, not definitive forensic proof. Intent rebuttal: comparator being a grievance note does not make released note a grievance; the released note’s context is a suspected hanging attempt plus “choose time to say goodbye” language, and the rule includes final-message/equivalent communication. No new evidence submitted because opponent’s evidence supplies targeted answer; use offered_evidence for D1-D3 in rebuttal.

Surrebuttal:defendant notes. Rescanned get_case/list_evidence. No new evidence submitted by plaintiff in rebuttal; rebuttal uses existing defense exhibits as PX-R1/R2/R3. Plaintiff points: SDNY order confirms public availability and cautious phrasing is enough; DOJ caveat only reduces certainty; AP handwriting analysis supplies affirmative credible reporting on authorship; intent follows from context, text, and repeated characterization; grievance comparator does not matter because one person can write both grievance and suicide/farewell notes. Response plan: concede publicity. Emphasize proposition/rule require not just public reporting of a label but qualifying authorship and intent. SDNY order is access ruling only; official source uses alleged/purported/reported wording and does not endorse authorship or intent. DOJ non-authentication matters because consensus of credible reporting remains unresolved when DOJ says it never had/authenticated the note. Handwriting AP is strongest plaintiff point, but it shows common authorship with a grievance note, not with authenticated Epstein samples; AP says none could definitively identify Epstein. Intent remains plaintiff's weakest element because the full text is grievance-like and the shared NO FUN phrase appears in a non-suicide grievance-note context; suspected-attempt context remains disputed by Epstein's reported denial and source-chain issues. No new outside search needed because rebuttal raised no new source, only interpretation of admitted evidence.

Closing work notes for plaintiff. Refreshed get_case/list_evidence. Final record contains original packet, three plaintiff submitted source captures (AP/PBS, Courthouse News, FOX Local), and three defense submitted sources (official SDNY order, AP DOJ-authentication caveat, AP handwriting analysis). Defendant surrebuttal concedes public availability/public reporting by May 8 but argues authorship and intent remain materially hedged. Closing theory: market rule is decisive: credible reporting consensus, no official-release or final forensic-authentication requirement. Public availability is proven and effectively conceded: SDNY order dated May 6 orders docketing/unsealing; AP/PBS/Courthouse/FOX all May 6-7. Authorship: defense’s own AP handwriting evidence affirmatively supports credible reporting because three forensic document examiners found common/appearing common authorship with the known public note found after Epstein’s death, one said same writer, and AP reported phrasing in the released note echoed Epstein’s prior emails. Intent/final-message: context is suspected hanging attempt, neck marks, suicide watch, and text about choosing time to say goodbye; even if not a classic suicide note, rule includes final message/equivalent. Caveats (DOJ had not authenticated, court no findings, Tartaglione source chain) reduce certainty but do not defeat preponderance under credible-reporting language. No new investigation in closing because closing must use existing record only.

Closing:defendant notes. Opportunity deliver_closing_statement only; no evidence submission. Rescanned get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff closing added no new evidence, only synthesis. Plaintiff closing: rule requires credible reporting not authentication; public availability undisputed via AP/PBS, Courthouse News, FOX Local, and SDNY order; authorship credibly reported by official order labels, AP/PBS caption, Courthouse/FOX labels, AP handwriting analysis common authorship plus Epstein phrase use; intent shown by suspected attempt context, goodbye language, NO FUN/NOT WORTH IT, repeated characterization, and broad final/equivalent wording. Defense closing plan: concede public availability and public label; focus on exact proposition and burden. Three failure points: cautious labels are not a preponderance finding that Epstein wrote it; source chain remains weak because DOJ never had/authenticated it and the court only ordered access; intent remains ambiguous because full text reads as grievance/frustration, comparator was a non-suicide grievance note, and Epstein reportedly denied self-harm. Handwriting evidence is strongest plaintiff point, but common authorship with a grievance note is not definitive Epstein authorship and does not prove suicide/final-message intent. Final ask: find proposition not proven; record proves a public release of a purported/alleged note, not a qualifying note under the market.
